label: GCP Cloud SQL MySQL
description: Connect to a Google Cloud SQL MySQL instance using host, port, credentials, and optional SSL.

form.title: Connect GCP Cloud SQL MySQL
form.subtitle: Enter your Cloud SQL instance connection details to start querying MySQL from Anton.

methods.label: Direct Connection
methods.description: Connect directly to your Cloud SQL MySQL instance using its public IP address, port, database name, username, and password. Optionally enforce SSL for encrypted connections.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Public IP (Direct Connection)

This is the simplest way to connect Anton to your Cloud SQL MySQL instance.

### Prerequisites

- Your Cloud SQL instance must have a **public IP** enabled.
- Your machine's IP must be added to the instance's **Authorized networks** list.

### Steps

1. Open [Google Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) and navigate to **SQL** in the left menu.
2. Click your MySQL instance to open its details page.
3. Under **Connectivity**, note the **Public IP address**. This is your host.
4. Go to **Connections → Networking → Authorized networks** and add your current public IP (or CIDR range) so Cloud SQL accepts inbound connections.
5. Under **Users**, confirm or create the MySQL user Anton will use. Note the username and password.
6. Enter the **Public IP** as the host, **3306** as the port (default), your **database name**, **username**, and **password** in the fields below.
7. If your instance requires SSL (recommended), toggle **Require SSL** on. Download the server CA cert from **Connections → Security** and paste it into the CA Certificate field.

### Notes

- Never add `0.0.0.0/0` to Authorized networks in production — whitelist only the IP running Anton.
- If you prefer not to expose a public IP, consider using the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy method instead.
- The default MySQL port is **3306** and is rarely changed.

fields.name: host
fields.label: Public IP Address
fields.description: The public IP of your Cloud SQL instance. Found in Cloud Console → SQL → your instance → Connectivity.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: MySQL port. Default is 3306 unless your instance is configured otherwise.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: The name of the MySQL database (schema) Anton should connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: MySQL user with access to the target database.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the MySQL user above.

fields.name: use_ssl
fields.label: Require SSL
fields.description: Enforce an SSL/TLS encrypted connection. Recommended for production instances.

fields.name: ssl_ca_cert
fields.label: SSL CA Certificate
fields.description: Server CA certificate in PEM format. Download from Cloud Console → SQL → your instance → Connections → Security. Only required when Require SSL is enabled.

methods.label: Cloud SQL Auth Proxy
methods.description: Route connections through the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy running locally. No public IP or Authorized networks needed — the proxy handles IAM-authenticated tunneling to Cloud SQL.
methods.how_to:
## Connect via Cloud SQL Auth Proxy

The Auth Proxy is Google's recommended approach when you don't want to expose a public IP or manage Authorized networks manually.

### Prerequisites

- [Download the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/sql-proxy#install) binary for your OS.
- A Google Cloud service account with the **Cloud SQL Client** (`roles/cloudsql.client`) IAM role.
- The service account's JSON key file.

### Steps

1. In [Cloud Console](https://console.cloud.google.com/) → **SQL**, click your instance and note the **Connection name** (format: `project:region:instance`).
2. Go to **IAM & Admin → Service Accounts**, create a service account, and grant it the **Cloud SQL Client** role.
3. Under the service account → **Keys** → **Add key → Create new key → JSON**. Download the JSON key.
4. Run the proxy locally:
   ```
   ./cloud-sql-proxy --credentials-file=/path/to/key.json PROJECT:REGION:INSTANCE
   ```
   By default it listens on `127.0.0.1:3306`.
5. In the fields below, set **Host** to `127.0.0.1`, **Port** to `3306`, and enter your database credentials.
6. SSL is handled by the proxy itself — leave **Require SSL** off when using this method.

### Notes

- The proxy must be running on the same machine as Anton for the connection to succeed.
- Keep the JSON key secret — it grants Cloud SQL access to everyone who holds it.
- See [Google's Auth Proxy guide](https://cloud.google.com/sql/docs/mysql/sql-proxy) for advanced flags (e.g. Unix socket mode).

fields.name: host
fields.label: Proxy Host
fields.description: Host where the Cloud SQL Auth Proxy is listening. Usually 127.0.0.1 when running locally.

fields.name: port
fields.label: Port
fields.description: Port the Auth Proxy is bound to. Default is 3306.

fields.name: database
fields.label: Database Name
fields.description: The name of the MySQL database (schema) Anton should connect to.

fields.name: user
fields.label: Username
fields.description: MySQL user with access to the target database.

fields.name: password
fields.label: Password
fields.description: Password for the MySQL user above.

fields.name: service_account_json
fields.label: Service Account JSON
fields.description: Optional. Paste the full contents of your service account JSON key if you want Anton to launch the proxy automatically. Leave blank if you're running the proxy yourself.
